headlines.summary: India is accelerating a plan to sell stakes in major state-held companies as it looks to raise billions of U.S. dollars to plug the gap left by the oil shock from the Strait of Hormuz closure. headlines.title: Saudi Arabia Rushes Crude Exports Through Reopened Strait of Hormuz
headlines.summary: About 10 million barrels of Saudi crude have cleared the Strait of Hormuz in recent days, as supertankers continue to load oil from the Saudi port of Ras Tanura in the Persian Gulf and Saudi Arabia is ramping up oil exports to Asia. headlines.title: Chinese Teapots Snap Up Middle East Crude As Prices Slide
headlines.summary: Chinese private refiners are buying Middle Eastern crude on the spot market amid falling prices, Bloomberg reported today, citing two purchases of Saudi and Emirati crude. headlines.title: Iran Exports Crude at 20% Premium Even as Unsold Barrels Pile Up at Sea
headlines.summary: Crude oil in floating storage off the Iranian coast is on the rise amid reluctance among buyers, Bloomberg reported today, citing data from Vortexa and its own calculations, suggesting some 58 million barrels are sitting on tankers. headlines.title: Same Fear, Different Fallout: Oil and Gold's Diverging Collapse
headlines.summary: Gold and oil spent five months moving like they were tied to the same rope. headlines.summary: Global investment firm KKR has agreed to acquire EDF Power Solutions North America in a landmark $4.2-billion deal, marking the largest single renewable energy investment in the company’s history.
